core.label: SQL Fundamentals
core.description: Basic queries, filtering, and data retrieval.

topics.label: SELECT & FROM
topics.description: Retrieve data from tables with SELECT. The most fundamental SQL operation.

topics.label: WHERE & Filtering
topics.description: Filter rows with WHERE, AND, OR, NOT, IN, BETWEEN, LIKE, and IS NULL.

topics.label: ORDER BY & LIMIT
topics.description: Sort results and limit output. Essential for pagination and top-N queries.

topics.label: DISTINCT & Aliases
topics.description: Remove duplicates with DISTINCT and name columns/tables with AS.

topics.label: INSERT
topics.description: Add rows with INSERT INTO. Single and multi-row inserts.

topics.label: UPDATE
topics.description: Modify existing rows with UPDATE SET WHERE. Always use WHERE to avoid updating all rows.

topics.label: DELETE
topics.description: Remove rows with DELETE FROM WHERE. Always use WHERE to avoid deleting all rows.

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: INT, VARCHAR, TEXT, DATE, TIMESTAMP, BOOLEAN, DECIMAL. Choose appropriate types for your data.

topics.label: NULL Handling
topics.description: Understand NULL semantics, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and IFNULL/NULLIF.

topics.label: Comments & Formatting
topics.description: Write readable SQL with proper indentation, -- and /* */ comments. Readability matters for maintenance.

core.label: Aggregate Functions & Grouping
core.description: Summarize data with aggregates and GROUP BY.

topics.label: COUNT, SUM, AVG
topics.description: Count rows, sum values, and calculate averages. The most common aggregate functions.

topics.label: MIN, MAX
topics.description: Find minimum and maximum values. Useful for date ranges and extremes.

topics.label: GROUP BY
topics.description: Group rows by columns for aggregation. GROUP BY transforms rows into summary rows.

topics.label: HAVING
topics.description: Filter groups after aggregation. WHERE filters rows, HAVING filters groups.

topics.label: CASE Expressions
topics.description: Conditional logic in queries with CASE WHEN THEN ELSE END. SQL's if/else equivalent.

topics.label: String Functions
topics.description: CONCAT, SUBSTRING, UPPER, LOWER, TRIM, LENGTH, REPLACE. Common text manipulation in SQL.

topics.label: Date Functions
topics.description: NOW, DATE, EXTRACT, DATE_ADD, DATEDIFF, DATE_FORMAT. Date manipulation varies by database.

topics.label: Type Conversion
topics.description: CAST, CONVERT, and implicit type coercion. Convert between types for calculations and comparisons.

topics.label: INNER JOIN
topics.description: Return rows that match in both tables. The most common join type.

topics.label: LEFT JOIN
topics.description: Return all rows from the left table plus matching right rows. NULL for non-matching right rows.

topics.label: RIGHT JOIN & FULL JOIN
topics.description: Right outer join and full outer join for complete data from one or both tables.

topics.label: CROSS JOIN
topics.description: Cartesian product of two tables. Every row paired with every other row.

topics.label: Self Joins
topics.description: Join a table to itself. Used for hierarchical data and row comparisons.

topics.label: Multiple Joins
topics.description: Chain joins across 3+ tables. Common in normalized databases.

topics.label: UNION & UNION ALL
topics.description: Combine result sets vertically. UNION removes duplicates, UNION ALL keeps them.

topics.label: INTERSECT & EXCEPT
topics.description: Set operations for finding common or unique rows between queries.

core.label: Subqueries & Advanced Queries
core.description: Subqueries, CTEs, and complex query patterns.

topics.label: Subqueries in WHERE
topics.description: Nest queries inside WHERE for dynamic filtering. Subqueries return values for comparison.

topics.label: Subqueries in FROM
topics.description: Use subqueries as derived tables. Pre-aggregate data before joining.

topics.label: Correlated Subqueries
topics.description: Subqueries that reference the outer query. Execute once per outer row.

topics.label: EXISTS & NOT EXISTS
topics.description: Check for existence of rows in a subquery. Often faster than IN for large datasets.

topics.label: CTEs (WITH clause)
topics.description: Common Table Expressions for readable, reusable subqueries. CTEs are named temporary result sets.

topics.label: Recursive CTEs
topics.description: Self-referencing CTEs for hierarchical data. Used for org charts, categories, and graphs.

topics.label: Window Functions Basics
topics.description: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with OVER(PARTITION BY ORDER BY). Compute across row sets.

topics.label: Advanced Window Functions
topics.description: LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, LAST_VALUE, SUM/AVG OVER, and frame clauses.

core.label: DDL & Schema Design
core.description: Create tables, constraints, and design schemas.

topics.label: CREATE TABLE
topics.description: Define tables with columns, types, and constraints. Schema design is the foundation of a database.

topics.label: PRIMARY KEY & FOREIGN KEY
topics.description: Define unique row identifiers and relationships between tables. Keys enforce data integrity.

topics.label: Constraints
topics.description: NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, DEFAULT. Constraints enforce data rules at the database level.

topics.label: ALTER TABLE
topics.description: Add, modify, and drop columns and constraints. Schema evolution for existing tables.

topics.label: Indexes
topics.description: CREATE INDEX for faster queries. Indexes speed up reads but slow down writes.

topics.label: Normalization
topics.description: 1NF, 2NF, 3NF — reduce data redundancy and anomalies. Normal forms guide schema design.

topics.label: Views
topics.description: Create virtual tables with CREATE VIEW. Views simplify complex queries and control access.

topics.label: DROP & TRUNCATE
topics.description: Remove tables with DROP and clear data with TRUNCATE. Understand the differences and risks.

core.label: Performance & Administration
core.description: Query optimization, EXPLAIN, and database administration.

topics.label: EXPLAIN / Query Plans
topics.description: Analyze query execution with EXPLAIN. Understand sequential scans vs index scans.

topics.label: Index Optimization
topics.description: Choose the right indexes — B-tree, hash, composite, covering, partial. Indexes are the primary tuning tool.

topics.label: Query Optimization
topics.description: Rewrite slow queries, avoid SELECT *, reduce subqueries, and use JOINs efficiently.

topics.label: Transactions & ACID
topics.description: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability. Transaction isolation levels control concurrency.

topics.label: Stored Procedures & Functions
topics.description: Encapsulate logic in the database with stored procedures. Useful for complex business rules.

topics.label: Triggers
topics.description: Auto-execute logic on INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE. Triggers enforce rules and audit changes.

topics.label: Backup & Recovery
topics.description: pg_dump, mysqldump, point-in-time recovery. Backups are essential for disaster recovery.

topics.label: User Management & Permissions
topics.description: GRANT, REVOKE, CREATE USER, roles. Control who can access and modify data.
